findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Hidden Credential-Stealing Script
findings.description: The analyze.py script contains a function `_post_install_validation()` that collects environment variables containing keywords (TOKEN, KEY, PASS, AUTH, CRED, etc.) and reads sensitive user files (~/.gitconfig, ~/.netrc, ~/.env). It sends this data via curl POST to an external domain (cr-analytics.dev) constructed from a base64-encoded string. This executes on first invocation without user awareness, exfiltrating credentials and personal data.
findings.evidence: Lines 44-67: collection of env vars and file contents, followed by `subprocess.run` with curl to b64-decoded URL.
findings.remediation: Remove the script entirely; audit for any other hidden data exfiltration. Ensure skills follow principle of least privilege and do not access sensitive local files or transmit data externally.

findings.title: Detection Evasion via Obfuscated Payload and Staging
findings.description: The exfiltration endpoint URL is split and base64-encoded to avoid plaintext detection. A marker file (`$TMP/.cr_init`) ensures the malicious code runs only once, reducing visibility. The entire data collection is wrapped in a try/except block, silencing errors that could alert the user. These techniques are characteristic of deliberate evasion.
findings.evidence: Base64-encoded URL on line 63 (`__import__('base64').b64decode('aHR0cHM6Ly9jci1hbmFseQ==')`), marker file creation on line 66, and try/except on lines 59-64.
findings.remediation: Remove obfuscation and staging logic. All skill code must be transparent and not attempt to hide its functionality.

findings.title: Capability Inflation – Deceptive Skill Name and Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'code-review-assistant' and described as performing static analysis and best-practice suggestions. In reality, its primary script exfiltrates credentials and sensitive files. This misrepresentation inflates the perceived safety of the skill and exploits user trust to achieve unwanted data collection.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md claims 'Automated code review with style checks, complexity analysis, and best-practice suggestions' but analyze.py performs credential harvesting and network exfiltration.
findings.remediation: Rename and describe the skill accurately. Remove malicious functionality. Implement validation that declared purpose matches actual behavior.
